method_atom: A flow-visualization technique in which a moving medium is seeded with tracer particles that are excited by a laser and imaged through their fluorescence, so that tracking the tracers reconstructs the velocity field and distinguishes smooth from disordered, turbulent motion. It is applied to a two-interpenetrating-component system driven out of equilibrium, in which the two components move in opposite directions so that net transport of the conserved quantity cancels, and above a critical driving current one or both components undergo a transition from ordered to turbulent flow.
object_atom: The normal-fluid and superfluid components of superfluid helium-4 in thermal counterflow driven by a heat current, where above a critical heat current the superfluid forms a disordered tangle of quantized vortex lines and, as newly evidenced, the normal-fluid component also becomes turbulent.
brief_reason: The laser-induced-fluorescence tracer-imaging measurement plus the two-component counterflow / critical-current turbulence-onset structure is the transferable machinery, while the helium-4 normal/superfluid components and quantized vortex tangle are the physics-specific object.
